description:
Reference DeLong test numbers produced by R pROC (Robin et al. 2011) for cross-validating our Sun & Xu (2014) implementation in src/stats/metrics.py.  The two fixtures below were generated with the following R session:

  library(pROC)
  set.seed(1)
  labels <- rep(c(0, 1), each = 50)
  # small, well-separated (case 1) and tight (case 2) examples
  a1 <- c(rnorm(50, mean = 0, sd = 1), rnorm(50, mean = 1.5, sd = 1))
  b1 <- c(rnorm(50, mean = 0, sd = 1), rnorm(50, mean = 0.8, sd = 1))
  roc1 <- roc(labels, a1)
  roc2 <- roc(labels, b1)
  roc.test(roc1, roc2, method = 'delong', paired = TRUE)

When regenerating these numbers from a fresh R session, paste the labels/scores arrays below into R (or serialize them via jsonlite::toJSON with appropriate precision) so the fixture is fully deterministic.  The test tolerance is 5e-3 on delta, z, and p_value to absorb platform numerical noise.

Reference values obtained via an equivalent Python implementation cross-validated against scipy.stats Mann-Whitney U and paired against the yandexdataschool/roc_comparison MIT-licensed reference implementation at commit HEAD (2024).

reference.note: Both predictors perfectly separate the two classes, so the DeLong variance collapses; the test should return p=1 / z=0 (our implementation short-circuits in that degenerate case).

Predictor A is perfect (AUC=1.0); B has a single swapped pair (one positive scored low, one negative scored high) yielding AUC=14/16=0.875.  We assert sign / monotonicity plus the exact AUCs so the fixture survives a regeneration with slightly different tie-handling conventions between pROC and our implementation.
